div.p: Message Schedule Expansion — lowercase sigma only

g.text: Why constants differ between σ0 and σ1
Schedule recurrence — how σ0 and σ1 combine (sha256_schedule.c)
σ0(x) = ROTR7 ⊕ ROTR18 ⊕ SHR3 (mod — no carry, pure XOR)
σ1(x) = ROTR17 ⊕ ROTR19 ⊕ SHR10 (mod — no carry, pure XOR)
σ0 constants | (7, 18, 3) | applied to W[t-15] in recurrence
σ1 constants | (17, 19, 10) | applied to W[t-2] in recurrence
σ0 ROTR gap | 18 - 7 = 11 | bits shifted between the two rotations
σ1 ROTR gap | 19 - 17 = 2 | NARROW gap — high positional overlap
σ0 SHR loss | 3 bits lost | mild truncation
σ1 SHR loss | 10 bits lost | stronger truncation for W[t-2]
Different constants ensure σ0(W[t-15]) and σ1(W[t-2]) have NO
overlapping bit-movement patterns → independent mixing channels
σ1(W[t-2]) | ROTR17^ROTR19^SHR10 | recent word — fast diffusion
σ0(W[t-15]) | ROTR7^ROTR18^SHR3 | older word — slow diffusion
W[t-16] | direct copy | far dependency (full period)
All four terms added mod 2^32 (uint32_t wraps automatically in C)
SHR(x,n) | x >> n | n bits destroyed — NOT invertible
ROTR(0x80000000,1) | 0x40000000 | top bit moved to pos 30
ROTR(0x00000001,1) | 0x80000000 | bit[0] moved to top — safe
WARNING: n must be 1..31 — n=0 or n=32 is undefined behavior
bits[31..25] | x[6..0] | LOW 7 bits wrap to TOP
bits[24..00] | x[31..7] | upper 25 bits shift down
NO bits lost — rotation preserves all 32
bits[31..14] | x[17..0] | LOW 18 bits wrap to TOP
bits[13..00] | x[31..18] | upper 14 bits shift down
NO bits lost — rotation preserves all 32
SHR(x, 3) — bits fall off right edge
bits[31..03] | x[31..3] | upper 29 bits shift down
bits[31..15] | x[16..0] | LOW 17 bits wrap to TOP
bits[14..00] | x[31..17] | upper 15 bits shift down
NO bits lost — rotation preserves all 32
bits[31..13] | x[18..0] | LOW 19 bits wrap to TOP
bits[12..00] | x[31..19] | upper 13 bits shift down
NO bits lost — rotation preserves all 32
SHR(x, 10) — bits fall off right edge
bits[31..10] | x[31..10] | upper 22 bits shift down
10 bits LOST — stronger truncation than σ0
